title: Create your first linear genome figure
summary: Upload one small phage GenBank record, separate the strands, show labels and a ruler, and export the result without running a sequence search.
requirements: No BLAST or LOSAT search is required.

downloads.label: NC_001416.1
downloads.note: Download the complete lambda phage record as a GenBank flat file.

manualSteps.title: Select Linear mode
manualSteps.body: Open gbdraw.app and select Linear before adding the phage record.

operations.body: Choose Linear; leave Pairwise Comparisons disabled for this one-record figure.

manualSteps.title: Upload the lambda GenBank record
manualSteps.body: Upload NC_001416.gb and set Output prefix to lambda_basic_linear. One record is enough to learn the linear layout controls.

operations.body: Keep a single Linear input row and verify the NC_001416.gb filename before continuing.

media.alt: A Linear GenBank input row showing the file and record text controls.
media.caption: Use one Linear input row; optional record text can be left blank.

manualSteps.title: Set labels, strands, and ruler
manualSteps.body: Enable Separate Strands, set Show Labels to All Records, set Scale Style to Ruler, and put the legend on the left.

operations.body: Choose Ruler (Ticks) from Scale Style after enabling labels and strand separation.

media.alt: Scale Style selector with Ruler highlighted in the linear layout controls.
media.caption: The ruler makes genomic positions explicit on the linear figure.

manualSteps.title: Generate and export SVG
manualSteps.body: Click Generate Diagram. Check that both strand lanes, feature labels, and the ruler are visible, then export SVG.

operations.body: Compare the generated preview with the Gallery result before exporting.

media.alt: Beginner linear lambda phage genome diagram with labels and a genomic ruler.
media.caption: The completed one-record linear figure requires no pairwise search.

related.label: Arrange linear tracks and record labels
